task: Implemented voice notification system with quota pre-warning, speak() sink gated by user preference, integration into stop/notification hooks, askr init prompt, comprehensive test coverage, and confirmed guard_runner.py notification.json path is dead code pending user direction on build-vs-cut decision.
discussion_summary: The voice notification feature is complete across 5 stages with all commits pushed and tests passing. This session independently verified (for the third time across three sessions) that guard_runner.py's non-blocking notification.json path (type: guard_warning) is dead code: pre_tool_use.py runs the guard check synchronously in-process and blocks via sys.exit(2), never spawning guard_runner.py or writing guard_trigger.json. A prior session opened then discarded a deletion goal within 4 minutes with no recorded rationale, so the decision to delete, wire up, or leave as documented dead code has been escalated to the user pending explicit direction rather than unilaterally resolved.

accomplishments.what: Added speak() sink function gated by voice_notifications preference
Wired speak() into stop.py notification hooks for task-done and HITL events
Added voice_notifications prompt to askr init flow
Implemented quota pre-warning at 75% threshold with quota_warned_sessions deduplication
Created test_voice.py with 11 tests covering speak() gating, config round-trip, and quota-warning scenarios
All 5 voice notification stages committed and pushed to main
Confirmed guard_runner.py notification.json path is dead code (three independent sessions reached same conclusion; prior deletion goal discarded within 4 minutes with no rationale recorded)
Recorded guard_runner.py dead-code finding as formal decision to prevent re-investigation in future sessions

next_actions.action: Get user decision on guard_runner.py: delete it, wire it up (spawn from pre_tool_use.py + write guard_trigger.json + whitelist guard_warning in extension.js), or leave as documented dead code. Asked via AskUserQuestion, no response received.
next_actions.why: Confirmed dead code (see Decisions) — this is a build-vs-cut product call, not something to resolve unilaterally

next_actions.action: Review and test the full voice notification flow end-to-end in a real session to confirm speak() is called at correct times and respects user preference
next_actions.why: Unit tests pass but integration with actual task lifecycle and notification events should be validated

decisions.decision: Voice notifications are gated by voice_notifications boolean preference stored in config
decisions.reason: Allows users to opt-in/out of audio feedback without code changes

decisions.decision: Quota pre-warning fires at 75% threshold and deduplicates via quota_warned_sessions set in lifecycle state
decisions.reason: Prevents spam of repeated warnings while giving user early notice before hitting hard limit

decisions.decision: speak() is a sink function that logs and returns early if voice_notifications is False
decisions.reason: Simplifies call sites — no conditional logic needed before calling speak()

decisions.decision: guard_runner.py non-blocking notification.json path (type: guard_warning) is confirmed dead code, not left unwired by accident: pre_tool_use.py already runs the guard check synchronously in-process and blocks via sys.exit(2), never spawning guard_runner.py or writing guard_trigger.json (the file check_and_save() expects). Left in place undeleted pending explicit user direction on build-vs-cut rather than unilaterally deleting or wiring it up.
decisions.reason: Three independent sessions now reached the same dead-code conclusion (goals 9ad0ffbf, 936fb14f); a prior session opened then discarded a deletion goal (af366587) within 4 minutes with no recorded rationale, so deleting without user sign-off risks redoing a call that was already reconsidered once

relational_files.why: Defines quota_warned_sessions state and lifecycle management for voice notifications
Wires speak() into task-done and HITL notification events
11 tests covering speak() gating, config round-trip, and quota-warning deduplication
Contains dead-code notification.json path (type: guard_warning) confirmed not invoked from pre_tool_use.py or HOOK_MAP
Runs guard checks synchronously in-process and blocks via sys.exit(2); never spawns guard_runner.py

blockers: User decision pending on guard_runner.py: delete, wire up, or leave as documented dead code
completed_goals: Verify guard_runner.py notification.json path is dead code or update HOOK_MAP
